name: NVIDIA H100 (Hopper)
key_insight: The GPU that defined the 2023-24 AI boom. 80GB HBM3, 3.35 TB/s bandwidth, ~4 PFLOPS FP8. Still the workhorse of the industry.

name: NVIDIA H200
key_insight: Hopper architecture with 141GB HBM3e at 4.8 TB/s. Same compute as H100, much more memory. China import-blocked Jan 2026.
notes: US cleared sales to 10 Chinese firms May 2026.

name: NVIDIA B200 (Blackwell)
key_insight: Flagship Blackwell. 192GB HBM3e, 8 TB/s, 4.5 PFLOPS FP8, 1,000W TDP. Two-die design. Shipping to hyperscalers since Nov 2024.

name: NVIDIA GB200 NVL72
key_insight: The liquid-cooled rack. 36 Grace CPUs + 72 Blackwell GPUs in a single NVLink domain. 13.4 TB HBM3e aggregate, 1,440 PFLOPS NVFP4 (sparse). Roughly $3M per rack.

name: NVIDIA Rubin R100
key_insight: Next-gen GPU on TSMC 3N, 338B transistors, 2,300W TDP, HBM4. Target H2 2026. Will require major facility redesigns.

name: NVIDIA DGX Cloud
key_insight: NVIDIA's own managed cloud running on partner infrastructure (CoreWeave, Oracle). DGX OS is a Linux distro optimized for NVIDIA hardware.

name: AMD MI300X
key_insight: 192GB HBM3, 5.3 TB/s, 5.22 PFLOPS FP8 (sparsity). The chip that lets a 70B model fit on a single GPU without tensor parallelism. The 192GB capacity is the moat vs H100.

name: AMD MI350X / MI355X
key_insight: CDNA4 architecture. 288GB HBM3e at 8 TB/s. Native FP4/FP6. MI355X at 1,400W TDP is the highest-TDP accelerator in market.

name: Intel Gaudi 3
key_insight: 128GB HBM2e, 1,835 TFLOPS FP8. Intel effectively shelved the Gaudi line in 2025; Habana business unit put up for sale.
notes: MEDIUM confidence - status needs primary verification.

name: Intel Xeon AMX
key_insight: 4th/5th Gen Xeon Scalable include Advanced Matrix Extensions (2D matrix registers + TMUL). Throughput is order of magnitude below GPU but eliminates GPU entirely. Popular for 'good enough' enterprise inference.

name: Google TPU v6e (Trillium)
key_insight: 4,614 TFLOPS FP8, 4.7 TOPS/W. Generally available Oct 2024. Pods of up to 256.

name: Google TPU 8i
key_insight: Announced Apr 2026. 288GB HBM3e, 10.1 PFLOPS FP4. First bifurcation of TPU into train (8t) vs infer (8i) SKUs. Collectives Acceleration Engine cuts sync latency 5x.

name: AWS Trainium2
key_insight: Powers 'Project Rainier' cluster for Anthropic. AWS uniquely secretive about Annapurna specs - no public FP8 TFLOPS, HBM capacity, or TDP. Trainium4 will license NVLink Fusion.

name: AWS Inferentia2
key_insight: Lower-cost inference alternative to NVIDIA. Available via Inf2 instances. ~190 TFLOPS FP8 (estimated).

name: Microsoft Maia 100
key_insight: TSMC 5nm, ~105B transistors, rumored 64GB HBM3. First Microsoft custom AI accelerator. Powers parts of Copilot and Azure OpenAI inference. Microsoft has never published official specs.

name: Meta MTIA v2
key_insight: Custom ASIC for Meta's ranking/recommendation inference. MTIA v2 announced 2024. Meta reportedly in talks Nov 2025 to deploy Google TPUs, suggesting MTIA isn't meeting frontier needs.

name: Groq LPU
funding: $640M Series D Aug 2024 at $2.8B; ~$20B NVIDIA licensing deal Dec 2025
key_insight: Language Processing Unit - SRAM-based, deterministic architecture. Claims 500 tok/s on Llama 3.3 70B and 1,000 tok/s on gpt-oss-20b. Revenue $500M in 2025.
notes: Saudi Arabia committed $1.5B for Dammam datacenter Feb 2025.

name: Cerebras CS-3 (WSE-3)
funding: Public May 2026 - IPO raised $5.55B at $185, $95B valuation
key_insight: Wafer-Scale Engine 3 - 4 trillion transistors, 900,000 cores, 5nm. Largest AI chip ever built. 2025 revenue $510M. Jan 2026: $10B OpenAI deal.

name: SambaNova SN40L RDU
funding: First close $1B at $11B valuation Jul 2026 (General Atlantic)
key_insight: Reconfigurable Dataflow Unit with three-tier memory. Marketing claim: Llama 3.1 405B on a single node. Sovereign AI partner network: Argyll/UK, Infercom/EU, OVHcloud/EU, SouthernCrossAI/AU.
notes: Intel reportedly signed term sheet to acquire.

name: Tenstorrent
key_insight: RISC-V + AI accelerators led by Jim Keller. Chiplet-based (Grayskull, Wormhole, Blackhole). Ethernet scaling instead of NVLink/InfiniBand. Open-source toolchain.

name: d-Matrix Corsair
key_insight: Chiplet + in-memory compute for low-precision (INT8/FP8) inference. Specifically targeted at inference, not training. Backed by Microsoft.

name: FuriosaAI Renegade
funding: TBA - reportedly rejected $800M Meta acquisition Feb 2025
key_insight: South Korea's leading AI chip startup. TSMC 5nm, 48GB HBM3, 2 PFLOPS FP8. Pursuing IPO rather than acquisition.
notes: Stalwart of the Korean silicon scene.

name: Rebellions Atom
key_insight: Korea. 24GB SRAM-only chip targeting tokens-per-watt leadership. ~30W for low-end LLM inference. Inference only.

name: NeuReality NR1
key_insight: Israel. 'AI-center-of-acceleration' focusing on CPU/network offload part of inference (the non-matmul parts). Claims 10x system efficiency.

name: Untether AI
key_insight: Canada. At-memory compute, ~1,000 TOPS claimed. Same chiplet-plus-SRAM thesis as d-Matrix but less famous.

name: Hailo Technologies
key_insight: Israel. Edge AI accelerators including a Raspberry Pi Hailo module (~26 TOPS at low power). Underexposed.

name: NVIDIA NVLink 5
key_insight: 1,800 GB/s (1.8 TB/s) on Blackwell. Up from 900 GB/s on Hopper. Tensor parallelism requires all-to-all low-latency communication per layer - this is the difference between 30 tok/s and 200+ on 70B+ models.

name: NVIDIA NVLink Fusion
key_insight: Announced 2025. Licensed to ARM, SiFive, and AWS (for Trainium4). First time NVIDIA has let non-NVIDIA chips use NVLink. Important erosion of NVIDIA exclusivity.

name: Ultra Ethernet Consortium (UEC)
funding: Consortium (AMD, Arista, Broadcom, Cisco, HPE, Intel, Meta, Microsoft)
key_insight: Founded July 2023. Goal: Ethernet-based competitor to InfiniBand for AI scale-out. Spec in development 2024-2026, early products shipping.

name: Crusoe Energy
key_insight: Powers datacenters with flared natural gas from oil wells. Subsidized by otherwise-wasted energy. Long-term availability is policy-dependent since flaring is a regulatory target.

name: Talen Energy / Susquehanna Nuclear
key_insight: $650M deal with AWS for dedicated datacenter campus at Talen's Susquehanna nuclear plant (Mar 2024). First direct 'nuclear plant to datacenter' co-location.

name: Constellation Energy / Three Mile Island Unit 1
key_insight: 20-year PPA with Microsoft (Sep 2024) to restart TMI-1 (closed 2019). Branded 'Crane Clean Energy Center.' Estimated restart cost ~$1.6B, target online 2028.

name: AWS (P5/P6/Inf2/Trn2)
key_insight: P5/P5e (8x H100/H200), P6 (8x B200), Inf2 (Inferentia2), Trn2 (Trainium2 for Anthropic 'Project Rainier'). EFA fabric up to 400 Gb/s.

name: Azure (ND H100 v5 / ND GB200 v6)
key_insight: ND H100 v5 GA. NDv5 H200 rolling out. ND GB200 v6 in preview. Tightest OpenAI integration via Azure OpenAI parity pricing. Maia100 instances for Copilot.

name: Google Cloud (A3/A4 + TPU)
key_insight: A3/A3 Mega/A3 Ultra (H100/H200), A4 (B200 limited). Plus entire TPU lineage. Only hyperscaler where you can run at scale on non-NVIDIA silicon and not feel it.

name: CoreWeave
key_insight: Dominant neo-cloud. Ex-crypto miner. First to cloud-deploy GB200 NVL72 (Feb 2025) and GB300 (Jul 2025). Customers: Microsoft, OpenAI ($12B/5yr), Meta ($21B), Anthropic. 2025 revenue $5.13B, net loss $1.2B. Q1 2026 revenue $2.08B.

name: Lambda
key_insight: Started as workstation builder for students. Now rents H100 ($3.99-$4.29/hr), H200, B200, A100, GH200. Favorite of ML researchers and indie developers.
notes: Formerly lambdalabs.com. Stalwart of affordable GPU access.

name: Nscale
key_insight: UK-based AI neocloud with aggressive European buildout. The European sovereign-AI play.

name: Genesis Cloud
key_insight: Germany-based, GDPR-friendly. Smaller but a real niche for European customers.

name: Modal
key_insight: Serverless-per-second Python compute. Cheapest true-serverless for spiky workloads. Per-CPU-cycle billing, no idle commitment. Free academic credits up to $10k.

name: Baseten
key_insight: Managed inference platform. Customer list reads like who's who of AI-native apps: Cursor, Notion, Descript, Sourcegraph, Harvey, Patreon. No idle billing. Public NVIDIA Dynamo user (2x TTFT).

name: Replicate
key_insight: Per-second billing for community + proprietary models. Powered by Cog (open-source packager). A100 80GB at $5.04/hr, H100 at $5.49/hr.

name: Beam
key_insight: Open-sourced core runtime. Modal-clone DX with multi-cloud support.

name: RunPod
key_insight: Two-tier trust model: Secure Cloud (H100 SXM $2.99/hr) and Community Cloud (H100 SXM $2.69/hr, RTX 4090 $0.34/hr). Lowest published GPU rates in the world.
notes: Stalwart - gateway drug for indie developers.

name: Vast.ai
key_insight: Peer-to-peer GPU marketplace. Popular with researchers and hobbyists. Structural analog to RunPod Community Cloud.

name: TensorDock
key_insight: B2B-focused spot marketplace. Early stage.

name: HPE Cray XD670 / EX
key_insight: HPC-heritage AI racks. HPE-Cerebras partnership for Leibniz Supercomputing Center.

name: SuperMicro HGX systems
key_insight: High-volume OEM of HGX H100/H200/B200 baseboards.

name: Dell PowerEdge XE9712
key_insight: First enterprise Blackwell server racks (Nov 2024). Dell-CoreWeave infrastructure partnership.

name: NVIDIA MIG
key_insight: Hardware-level partitioning of one GPU into up to 7 isolated instances (H100: 7x10GB; H200: 7x18GB). True hardware isolation. Unavailable on consumer GeForce cards by NVIDIA policy.

name: NVIDIA Run:ai
key_insight: Kubernetes-native GPU scheduler. MIG-aware fractional GPU scheduling, gang scheduling, fair-share. De-facto standard for AI cluster sharing.

name: Volcano
key_insight: Batch and gang-scheduling for Kubernetes. Essential for distributed inference pods that need many GPUs atomically.

name: Kueue
key_insight: Default batch queuing in K8s 1.30+. The boring standard for non-gang workloads.

name: Ray Serve
key_insight: Production model serving with autoscaling. Widely used for offline batch inference at scale (ByteDance 200TB multi-modal case).

name: Anyscale
key_insight: The company behind Ray. Fully-managed Ray, plus Anyscale Endpoints inference API (now somewhat deprecated as standalone).

name: KServe
key_insight: Knative-based, scale-to-zero, InferenceService CRD. Autoscaling 5x faster than raw HPA per community benchmarks. The boring standard choice.

name: Seldon Core 2
key_insight: K8s-native, repositioned around 'AI governance infrastructure' in 2025. Supports vLLM and HF backends.

name: BentoML / Yatai / BentoCloud
key_insight: Python-native model packager ('Bento' archive format). Yatai is K8s control plane. BentoCloud is managed version.

name: NVIDIA NIM
key_insight: Containerized inference microservices. Model weights + runtime + monitoring in one Docker. ~100+ models. License is NVIDIA AI Enterprise (commercial). Engines inside are TensorRT-LLM and vLLM.

name: NVIDIA Dynamo
key_insight: Headline 2025 release. Rust + Python. Orchestration layer above engines - disaggregated prefill/decode, KV-aware routing, KVBM (GPU->CPU->SSD KV offload), ModelExpress (7x cold-start). 7.5k GitHub stars.
notes: Production: 7x throughput on DeepSeek-R1/GB200 (SemiAnalysis), 2x TTFT (Baseten), 80% fewer SLA breaches (Alibaba).

name: NVIDIA Triton Inference Server
key_insight: Now branded Dynamo-Triton. Backend-agnostic (TensorRT, PyTorch, ONNX, OpenVINO). Multi-model serving - picks up where dedicated LLM engines stop (rerankers, embedders, preprocessors).

name: ONNX Runtime
key_insight: Cross-platform ML runtime. The enterprise 'interoperable' default.

name: OpenAI Triton (language)
key_insight: GPU kernel DSL. NOT a server. vLLM, TensorRT-LLM, FlashAttention, and SGLang sgl-kernel all emit Triton kernels. The hidden layer beneath the engines.

name: vLLM
key_insight: 86.7k GitHub stars. PagedAttention + continuous batching + prefix caching + speculative decoding. Default for any production GPU serve. Supports NVIDIA, AMD, Intel Gaudi, TPU, CPU, Neuron, Ascend, Apple Silicon.
notes: Born from SOSP 2023 paper from UC Berkeley Sky Computing Lab.

name: SGLang
key_insight: 30.5k GitHub stars. RadixAttention (radix-tree KV cache reuse) - up to 6.4x throughput on agentic/structured workloads. Best for structured generation (JSON/tool calls). Adopters: xAI, AMD, NVIDIA, Cursor, Azure, AWS.

name: llama.cpp
key_insight: 121k GitHub stars. GGUF format + k-quants. Runs on CPU, CUDA, Metal, ROCm, Vulkan, SYCL, CANN, WebGPU. The most-deployed inference engine in the world by install count. Foundation of Ollama, LM Studio, gpt4all, llamafile.
notes: THE Stalwart. Built solo by Georgi Gerganov.

name: Ollama
key_insight: 176k GitHub stars. DX wrapper around llama.cpp. Local runtime free + OSS; cloud upsell at $20/mo Pro or $100/mo Max. The local product is the moat; the cloud is the revenue.
notes: 'ollama run llama3.2' is the first command new AI developers learn.

name: LM Studio
key_insight: Most polished GUI for downloading, managing, and chatting with local models. Built on top of llama.cpp. Recently launched 'LM Studio Bionic' - pivot from model runner to agent runtime.

name: TensorRT-LLM
key_insight: 14.2k GitHub stars. NVIDIA's performance play for H100/H200/B100/B200. Kernel fusion, in-flight batching, FP8/FP4 inference. Fully open-sourced March 2025. Public adopters: Databricks, Snowflake, Microsoft Bing.
notes: Trade-off: NVIDIA-only, slower to add new model architectures.

name: Hugging Face TGI
key_insight: 10.9k stars. ARCHIVED March 21, 2026. HF now recommends vLLM, SGLang, llama.cpp, or MLX instead. Lesson: even well-funded OSS does not survive if it loses the technical race.

name: MLX
key_insight: 27.6k GitHub stars. Apple's array framework for unified memory. Most-used inference engine on Mac, by far. Recently added CUDA and CPU Linux support. Every Mac is a viable local-inference machine because of MLX.
notes: Silent winner of the local-inference tier.

name: ExLlamaV3
key_insight: Fastest engine for quantized models on consumer NVIDIA GPUs (RTX 3090/4090). EXL2 mixed-bit quantization - 4.5-5 bpw average. V2 archived mid-2025; V3 ongoing.

name: LMDeploy
key_insight: 8k stars. TurboMind (C++) + PyTorch backends. Strong on Chinese models (InternVL, Qwen) and Chinese hardware (Ascend, Cambricon). Underappreciated outside China.

name: Mooncake
key_insight: 5.9k stars. KVCache-centric disaggregated architecture. FAST 2025 Best Paper. Transfer Engine delivers 87-190 GB/s on 4x200/8x400 Gbps RoCE. Reference architecture for 2026 SOTA. Production at Kimi/Moonshot (224k prefill tok/s, 288k decode tok/s on 128x H200).
notes: Integrated into vLLM, SGLang, TensorRT-LLM, LMDeploy.

name: llamafile
key_insight: Single-file distributable LLM using Cosmopolitan libc. Cross-platform (Win/macOS/Linux/BSD). Cleanest abstraction for 'ship a model as a binary.'

name: xgrammar
key_insight: Constrained decoding library. 3.5x faster than outlines on JSON, 10x on CFG. Current speed leader. Used by SGLang and vLLM via pluggable backends.
notes: Stalwart - underused outside the major engines.

name: NIXL
key_insight: NVIDIA Inference Transfer Library. The abstraction that Dynamo and Mooncake both plug into for cross-node KV cache transfer. Small, focused, doing one job exceptionally well.

name: LiteLLM
key_insight: 54,054 stars, active daily. Rust core + Python SDK. Routes to 100+ providers behind OpenAI-compatible interface. Adopters: Stripe, Netflix, OpenAI Agents SDK, Google ADK, OpenHands. De facto open-source winner.
notes: Implicated in Mercor supply-chain breach Apr 2026 - now critical-infra attack surface.

name: OpenRouter
key_insight: Largest model marketplace. 400+ models from 70+ providers, 5.5% fee on PAYG. Weekly 25T tokens, 5x YoY. Co-founded by OpenSea founders. Default 'model dropdown' for indie AI apps.
notes: Stalwart - in commoditizing market, the marketplace is the moat.

name: Vercel AI SDK
key_insight: 25,669 stars. TypeScript-first router. Default for Next.js ecosystem. Less feature-complete than LiteLLM but tightly integrated into Vercel deployment story.

name: Portkey
key_insight: 12,471 stars, MIT. Gateway + guardrails (1,600+ LLMs, 50+ guardrails). MIT-licensed gateway is wedge; paid observability/control plane is the business.

name: Helicone
key_insight: 5,970 stars, Apache-2.0. Hybrid observability + proxy.

name: Cloudflare AI Gateway
key_insight: Edge proxy for AI APIs. Bundled with Cloudflare Workers AI.

name: OpenAI API
key_insight: GPT-5.6 family spans $1.10/$6.60 (Luna) to $5.50/$33.00 (Sol). Notably also offers gpt-oss open-weight models at parity with Groq - frontier lab voluntarily commoditizing its own low end.
notes: Pricing via AWS Bedrock passthrough; direct pricing page gated.

name: Anthropic API
key_insight: Pricing ranges from $1/$5 per million tokens (Haiku 4.5) up to $10/$50 (Fable 5). Most aggressive prompt-caching economics in frontier tier - separate 5-min vs 1-hour TTLs.
notes: Tools: web search $10/1K, code exec $0.05/hr, Managed Agents $0.08/session-hour.

name: Google Gemini API
key_insight: Gemini 3.5 Flash, Gemini 3.1 Pro shipping. Per-hour cache-storage fee on top of cache-hit pricing; tiered pricing above 200K tokens.
notes: Direct pricing page timed out in research - LOW confidence on specific numbers.

name: xAI Grok API
key_insight: Grok 4.5 pricing includes long-context surcharge: $2/$6 below 200k context, $4/$12 above. Voice realtime at $0.05/min. Aggressive multimodal pricing (image, video).

name: Cohere
key_insight: Only frontier lab purpose-built for enterprise retrieval. Model Vault dedicated single-tenant product: Embed 4 Small $4/hr, Rerank 4 Pro Large $10/hr.

name: Together AI
key_insight: Open-model inference + dedicated + GPU clusters + fine-tuning. Custom Together Inference Engine 2.0 built on FlashAttention-3 (Tri Dao team). Llama-3 API pioneer.

name: DeepInfra
key_insight: Low-cost per-token inference. A100 dedicated at $0.89/hr - among the cheapest dedicated rates in the market. H100 $2.20/hr, B200 $3.69/hr.

name: Fireworks AI
key_insight: Serverless open-model inference + on-demand GPU + fine-tuning. Recently crossed $1B ARR (self-reported). Also resold inside Azure AI Foundry - unusual 'aggregator-of-aggregators' inversion.
notes: ARR is self-reported, treat as MEDIUM confidence.

name: AWS Bedrock
key_insight: Carries 18 model providers including OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, xAI - direct competitors to each other. Most multi-vendor of hyperscaler gateways. Service tiers: Standard, Priority (+75%), Flex (-50%), Reserved.

name: Azure AI Foundry
key_insight: Formerly Azure OpenAI. 11,000+ model catalog. Two deployment modes: Managed Compute (dedicated GPU per hour) and Serverless (per-token). Includes Fireworks as sub-provider. PTUs flex across OpenAI and DeepSeek.

name: Google Vertex AI
key_insight: Direct access to Gemini models and Google TPUs. Integration with GCP IAM and BigQuery.

name: Stripe Metered Billing
key_insight: Supercharged by Metronome acquisition Dec 2025. Default for AI metering. The standalone billing category was acquired out of existence in 6 months.

name: Lago
key_insight: 10,236 stars, AGPL-3.0, active. Leading independent metering/billing option post-consolidation. OSS, payment-processor-neutral. The Stalwart survivor of the 2026 billing consolidation.

name: Metronome (acquired by Stripe)
key_insight: Last independent val. ~$470M / $128M raised. Customers: OpenAI, Anthropic, Databricks, NVIDIA. Ex-Dropbox founders.

name: Orb (acquired by Adyen)
key_insight: Customers: Vercel, Replit, Supabase, Glean. All-cash deal announced Jun 11, 2026.

name: Unkey
key_insight: 5,397 stars, custom license, active daily. API-key management + rate limiting. Go. Clean, focused primitive.
notes: Stalwart of the API key primitive.

name: tiktoken
key_insight: 18,782 stars, MIT. De-facto token counter. Foundation library that every metering system leans on. Most-deployed library in this entire layer by install count.

name: Hugging Face tokenizers
key_insight: 10,896 stars, Apache-2.0, Rust core. The other major tokenization library.

name: Langfuse
funding: Commercial entity acquired by ClickHouse Jan 2026 (with $400M Series D @ $15B)
key_insight: 31,449 stars, active daily. Commercial entity acquired by ClickHouse Jan 2026; OSS repo remains hyperactive. The 'acquired company, living project' pattern - track repo pulse, not corporate owner.

name: promptfoo
key_insight: 23,420 stars, MIT, active daily. Red-teaming + eval. 'Used by OpenAI and Anthropic' per README. Quietly one of the most strategically positioned OSS projects in this layer.

name: Arize Phoenix
key_insight: 10,633 stars. Leading *independent* eval platform post-Langfuse-acquisition.

name: ragas
key_insight: 14,914 stars. RAG evaluation standard. Repo moved from explodinggradients to vibrantlabsai.

name: OpenLLMetry
key_insight: 7,311 stars, Apache-2.0. OpenTelemetry-native. The 'boring, interoperable' enterprise choice. Wins deployments that refuse proprietary tracing.

name: W&B Weave
key_insight: 1,108 stars (low signal). Commercial-first; benefits from W&B's massive distribution.

name: Braintrust
key_insight: Commercial eval platform, active. SDK stars aren't a useful signal.

name: LangSmith
key_insight: Commercial observability/play from the LangChain team. Bundled with LangChain ecosystem.

name: Artificial Analysis
key_insight: De facto industry scorecard. Independent, methodology public. Measures P50 over 72 hours across first-party APIs and 19 third-party providers. Tracks 576+ models and 19 API providers. Benchmark everyone in the market quietly checks.

name: LMSYS Chatbot Arena
key_insight: 39,492 stars (FastChat repo). Crowdsourced blind pairwise ranking. Gold standard for 'which model is smarter.' Academic/nonprofit governance gives it authority vendor benchmarks lack.

name: MLPerf Inference
key_insight: Audited hardware benchmark with strict submission rules. Four scenarios: SingleStream, MultiStream, Server (throughput under latency constraint), Offline (max throughput). Highest credibility for hardware/system claims.

name: Aider LLM Leaderboard
key_insight: Coding-specific quality ranking. The benchmark practitioners actually trust for 'which model writes the best code.'

name: Cline
key_insight: 64,821 stars, Apache-2.0, active daily. Largest OSS coding agent by stars. Dark-horse winner of 2025-26. Bigger than most infrastructure projects beneath it.

name: Aider
key_insight: 47,523 stars, Apache-2.0. Terminal pair-programmer. Runs the Aider LLM Leaderboard.

name: Continue
key_insight: 34,977 stars. OSS coding agent. Multi-model by design - major gateway demand driver.

name: Helion Energy
key_insight: Fusion energy startup. Signed Microsoft PPA. If fusion works at commercial scale, the power constraint on AI inference changes overnight.

name: Apple Neural Engine / M-series
key_insight: ANE in A-series/M-series SoCs (16-35 TOPS INT8). M3 Ultra: 192GB unified memory. The 'consumer inference sleeper' - a Mac desktop can run Llama 3 70B in BF16 entirely in RAM.

name: Huawei Ascend 910C
key_insight: The first Chinese AI accelerator to reach rough H100 parity. SMIC N+2 process, 2x 910B chiplets, ~60% of H100 inference throughput per DeepSeek researchers. Mass-shipped May 2025. 800k+ 910B/910C units expected in 2025.
notes: Powers CloudMatrix 384 rack system. DeepSeek R1 inference runs on it.

name: Huawei Ascend 920
key_insight: Announced April 2025 as H20 replacement after US export controls banned H20 from China. SMIC 6nm, ~900 TFLOPS/card, HBM3 at 4 TB/s. Mass production late 2025.

name: Huawei CloudMatrix 384
key_insight: The rack-scale answer to NVIDIA NVL72. 384 Ascend 910C chips across 16 racks with 6,972 x 800G LPO optical transceivers. Beats NVL72 in absolute perf, 2.3x worse perf-per-watt. RMB 60M (~$8.2M) per set.

name: CANN
key_insight: Compute Architecture for Neural Networks - Huawei's CUDA equivalent. Required dependency for everything above (vllm-ascend requires CANN 9.0.1). Closed-source, unlike CUDA. The single biggest reason Ascend adoption outside China is hard.

name: torch_npu
key_insight: PyTorch backend for Ascend. Uses PrivateUse1 dispatch key. TorchNPU 26.0.0 supports PyTorch 2.10.0. THE foundational layer for open-source on Ascend - every other framework sits on top.
notes: Stalwart - the bridge between PyTorch ecosystem and Chinese silicon.

name: MindSpore
key_insight: Huawei's PyTorch alternative. Apache-2.0. Source on Gitee not GitHub (structural finding - Chinese ecosystem runs on Chinese forges). Used for PanGu-Sigma. Losing framework war to PyTorch globally but still native path for PanGu.

name: Cambricon
key_insight: Pure-play Chinese AI chip designer. 2024 revenue CN¥1.18B, first profit late 2024 after 7 years of losses. 2024 stock +383% - China's top-performing stock of the year. Originally supplied NPUs to Huawei for Kirin 970 - Huawei in-housed in 2018.

name: Biren Technology
key_insight: Founded 2019 by ex-NVIDIA Lingjie Xu. BR100 (77B transistors, 2 PFLOPS claimed) on TSMC 7nm pre-sanctions. TSMC halted fabbing after Oct 2022 controls. Modified to evade thresholds. Added to Entity List Oct 2023.
notes: Canonical example of sanctions reshaping Chinese AI silicon.

name: Moore Threads
key_insight: Founded Oct 2020 by ex-NVIDIA China VP Zhang Jianzhong. TOP INVESTOR: Liang Wenfeng, DeepSeek/High-Flyer founder. MTT S60/S80/S2000/S4000 lineup. MUSA software stack (CUDA-like). 12nm process.
notes: DeepSeek founder's investment is the strategically interesting signal.

name: MetaX
key_insight: Founded Sept 2020 by ex-AMD engineers. C500 (7nm, ~75% of A100 perf) - CUDA-COMPATIBLE (the main differentiator). 9 compute clusters across China, 10k+ GPUs by end of 2024.
notes: Stalwart - only credible drop-in NVIDIA alternative from Chinese vendor.

name: Hygon
key_insight: 2016 AMD JV - licensed Zen 1 x86 IP for $293M. Only Chinese company that can legally produce x86 CPUs. Entity-Listed June 2019. DCU GPU line is AMD CDNA-derived, ~MI100 class. 2023 revenue CN¥6.01B.

name: CXMT
key_insight: ChangXin Memory Technologies - China's only DRAM/HBM hope. 4% of global DRAM market Q2 2025. Aiming to begin HBM production by end of 2026. THE critical bottleneck for Chinese AI chips.
notes: Q1 2026 revenue 50.8B yuan (+700% YoY). US DoD Chinese military companies list June 2026.

name: SMIC
key_insight: Makes Ascend 910B (N+1 process) and 910C (N+2 process). N+2 is SMIC's 2nd-gen 7nm-equivalent achieved without EUV. The manufacturing limit of Chinese-domestic fab capability today.

name: MAAS (Canonical)
key_insight: Metal-as-a-Service - de facto standard for bare-metal provisioning. IPMI/Redfish-based. Free / $30 / $50 per machine/year. Customers: T-Mobile, AT&T, Verizon, NTT, BT, Telefonica, Barclays, Sandia, Ubisoft, Roblox, EA.

name: Tinkerbell
key_insight: K8s-native bare-metal workflow. Components: Smee (DHCP/tftp), Tootles (iPXE), Hook (OS installer), Tink (workflow engine), Rufio (BMC controller), CAPT (Cluster API provider). Open-source purist's choice.

name: Talos Linux
key_insight: Immutable, API-only Linux for Kubernetes. No SSH, no shell. Image-based updates. Emerging as default for sovereign/edge. Six-person Sidero Labs team. Conceptual move from 'pet Linux server I SSH into' to 'cattle Linux image I declare in YAML.'

name: NVIDIA BlueField DPU
key_insight: The dominant DPU. BlueField-4 (Oct 2025) gives 6x compute and 800 Gb/s. In CoreWeave's production model, the BlueField DPU (not the hypervisor or GPU) is the multi-tenant trust boundary. Becoming the default sovereign-cloud isolation primitive via DPF Operator.

name: NVIDIA Confidential Computing
key_insight: Hardware-isolated TEE on Hopper, extending to Vera Rubin. Tenant code and model weights protected even from host operator. PPCIE mode for multi-GPU trust domains. Adopters: Google Cloud, Azure, Anthropic, xAI, Apple Private Cloud Compute. Disables MIG (mutually exclusive).
notes: 3rd gen extends across whole NVL72 rack.

name: NVIDIA DSX
key_insight: 2026 full AI factory platform. DSX Reference Design + DSX Sim + DSX OS + DSX MaxLPS + DSX Flex + DSX Exchange. Partners: IREN (5 GW), Jacobs, Phaidra, Schneider/EcoDataCenter, Emerald AI. Canonical sovereign-AI reference architecture.

name: WekaIO
key_insight: Software-defined parallel FS for AI workloads. ~80 GB/s per cluster. Strong AI story; commonly paired with NVIDIA SuperPODs.

name: VAST Data
key_insight: Disaggregated shared-everything architecture. Expensive but excellent at scale. Often paired with NVIDIA SuperPODs.

name: NVIDIA Magnum IO / GPUDirect Storage
key_insight: NVIDIA's storage optimization stack. GPUDirect Storage bypasses host CPU for direct GPU-to-NVMe loading - critical for fast cold-starts.

name: Cilium
key_insight: eBPF-based CNI and security layer. Becoming default AI-cluster CNI because it handles high-throughput east-west traffic well. Layer 7 awareness. CNCF Graduated.

name: VictoriaMetrics
key_insight: Prometheus-compatible metrics database. 10x lower resource use than Prometheus+Thanos at comparable query performance. Increasingly the production choice for high-cardinality AI workloads.

name: NVIDIA DCGM Exporter
key_insight: Canonical GPU metrics exporter for Prometheus. Exposes GPU utilization, memory, temperature, power, ECC errors. Required baseline for any production GPU observability stack.

name: HashiCorp Vault / OpenBao
key_insight: Vault (HashiCorp) is de facto secrets management standard. After IBM acquired HashiCorp 2024 and Vault moved to BSL license, OSS community forked into OpenBao under Linux Foundation. OpenBao is the sovereign-cloud answer.
notes: Stalwart - critical primitive kept in OSS.

name: Keycloak
key_insight: Open-source Identity and Access Management. SAML, OIDC. The sovereign-cloud default. Red Hat sponsors. CNCF Incubating.

name: llm-d
key_insight: 3.8k stars, v0.8.1 (June 2026), Apache-2.0. CNCF Sandbox (March 2026). Founded by Red Hat, Google Cloud, IBM Research, CoreWeave, NVIDIA. The multi-vendor coalition's coordinated counter to NVIDIA Dynamo.
notes: Supporters: AMD, Cisco, Hugging Face, Intel, Lambda, Mistral AI, UC Berkeley, U Chicago.

name: KAI Scheduler
key_insight: Originally Run:ai proprietary gang scheduling code. Open-sourced by NVIDIA post-acquisition under ai-dynamo org. Topology-aware placement. Part of NVIDIA Dynamo stack.

name: Grove
key_insight: K8s operator for topology-aware gang scheduling - purpose-built for NVL72. Critical because NVL72 changes unit of scheduling from '8 GPUs in a node' to '72 GPUs in a rack.' Part of NVIDIA Dynamo.

name: vLLM-Ascend
key_insight: 2.4k stars, 1.7k forks, v0.18.0 (April 2026). Production-ready vLLM plugin for Huawei Ascend. Supports DeepSeek V3/R1, LLaMA, embedding, multi-modal. Implements vLLM Hardware Pluggable RFC #11162.
notes: Stalwart - the bridge that makes open-source inference portable to Chinese silicon.

name: Argyll DataCloud
key_insight: UK-based sovereign AI cloud. SambaNova sovereign partner. Part of the four-partner SambaNova sovereign network (Argyll UK, Infercom EU, OVHcloud EU, SouthernCrossAI AU).

name: OVHcloud
key_insight: Independent European cloud. SambaNova sovereign partner for EU. GDPR-friendly. The European sovereign-AI alternative to US hyperscalers.

name: SouthernCrossAI
key_insight: Australia-based sovereign AI cloud. SambaNova sovereign partner. The Australian entry in the SambaNova sovereign network.

name: Red Hat OpenShift AI
key_insight: Enterprise AI platform on OpenShift. Multi-vendor hardware (NVIDIA, AMD, Intel). Includes Open Data Hub. The choice for enterprises that want to avoid full NVIDIA lock-in and are already invested in Red Hat.

name: Dell AI Factory
key_insight: Dell + NVIDIA partnership. PowerEdge XE9712 first enterprise Blackwell server racks (Nov 2024). Turnkey AI factory for enterprise customers.

name: NVIDIA Spectrum-X Ethernet
key_insight: NVIDIA's Ethernet-for-AI play, acknowledging UEC's existence. Pitched as InfiniBand-like lossless Ethernet. Bundled with BlueField DPUs as turnkey fabric. Some large CoreWeave deployments use it.

name: NVIDIA UFM
key_insight: Unified Fabric Manager - de facto InfiniBand management tool. Proprietary. NO widely-adopted open-source equivalent exists - this is a Stalwart opportunity.
